Off Calendar May-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Shoda of Pennington Lawson LLP telephone number 415-484-4343 for Plaintiff William Ryder.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative rulimg. CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM RYDER'S DEMURRER TO the sole claim for money had and received in the cross-complaint of Satel, LLC and Richard Hylen is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The sole cross-claim necessarily depends on reformation of the 2006 promissory note and thus cross-complainants must allege all the required elements of a claim for reformation, which they have not done. Moreover, even without granting cross-defendant's request for judicial notice, the cross-claim on its face appears to be time-barred. If they can do so in good faith, cross-complainants are granted leave to amend to allege all the elements of a claim for reformation and to allege facts which show that the cross-claim did not accrue until some date later than 2006 and is not time-barred. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Evidence
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RYAN WERNER of Pennington Lawson, Tel No. (415) 484-4344 appearing for the Plaintiff William F. RYDER;
Atty. BROCK R. LYLE of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, Tel. No. (650) 714-9945 appearing for Defendants Satel LLC and Richard Hyler.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF WILLIAM RYDER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are triable issues as to both the principal amount and the interest rate of the promissory note of January 7, 2003 as referenced in both the Operating Agreement and Addendum No. 1 to the Operating Agreement. The promissory note of January 7, 2003 is not part of the evidentiary record on this motion and thus the terms of that note must be ascertained, if at all, by other evidence. While the Agreement for Sale of Business signed May 24, 2005 has been presented as evidence of the terms of the January 7, 2003 note, that agreement does not state that it is re-stating the terms of the January 7, 2003 note, nor is that agreement referenced in either the Operating Agreement or Addendum No. 1 to the Operating Agreement, even though the Agreement for Sale of Business predates the Operating Agreement. Neither the Operating Agreement or Addendum No. 1 states the interest rate of the January 7, 2003 note, so parol evidence is permissible on that issue, and defendants have presented admissible evidence that the 4% interest rate 4% as claimed by plaintiff is disputed. Since the 4% rate is central to plaintiff's calculations of the amounts owed to him by defendants and sought by this motion, the triable issue as to the interest rate alone is sufficient to deny the motion. More fundamentally though, liberally construing defendants' evidence, that evidence shows that there was a mistake in documenting the terms of the January 7, 2003 note, even assuming that that note stated terms of $1,000,000 principal and 4% interest, which has not been shown. The parole evidence rule, as codified in this state, does not preclude evidence to show that the terms of an integrated written contract. (CCP 1856(e)). Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of Ms. Stameroff are overruled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM RYDER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT (of Satel, LLC and Richard Hylen).
Appearances: Alex Merchant of the Tobener Law Center (no phone number provided) for cross-defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained without leave to amend as to the first and third causes of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. The first and third claims are deficient as a matter of law and it does not appear likely that cross-complainants can cure the deficiencies. The first claim does not allege that Mr. William Ryder committed or threatened to commit an unlawful act. The third claim fails because the covenant of good faith does not extend to refraining of participating in the alleged conspiracy and there can be no liability for treating different defendants disparately. Cross-complainants are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege all of the elements of a fraud claim with particularity. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
